card.hook: A smart-contract warning extension that matters at signing time cannot depend on remote executable updates: Manifest V3 requires packaged code, while the main theft pattern is approval and typed-signature abuse rather than key theft. That creates a hard design constraint: local decoding must cover calldata, EIP-712 permits, and ERC-1967 proxy resolution well enough to classify risk before signature.
card.why_it_matters: If you are learning to build browser-extension security for smart contracts, this gives you the minimum technical scope for a useful signer-side classifier instead of a generic transaction decoder.
card.action: Write a 3-bullet design note that maps one local MV3 check to each signing surface named in the article: calldata selector decoding, EIP-712 parsing of chainId and verifyingContract, and ERC-1967 implementation-slot lookup.
